Apple___Apple_scab.treatment: Apply captan or myclobutanil fungicide. Remove fallen infected leaves. Prune trees for better air circulation.

Apple___Black_rot.treatment: Remove mummified fruits and cankers. Apply captan or thiophanate-methyl fungicide during bloom.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.treatment: Apply myclobutanil or mancozeb fungicide in spring. Remove nearby juniper/cedar trees if possible.

Apple___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Continue regular care with proper watering and fertilization.

Blueberry___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Maintain acidic soil pH (4.5-5.5) and regular mulching.

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.treatment: Apply sulfur-based or potassium bicarbonate fungicide. Ensure good air circulation by pruning.

Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Continue proper care with adequate sunlight and watering.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.treatment: Apply strobilurin or triazole fungicide. Rotate crops and use resistant hybrids. Tillage helps reduce inoculum.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.treatment: Apply mancozeb or propiconazole fungicide if infection is severe. Plant rust-resistant varieties.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.treatment: Apply propiconazole or azoxystrobin fungicide. Use resistant hybrids and rotate crops.

Corn_(maize)___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Maintain proper nitrogen fertilization and irrigation.

Grape___Black_rot.treatment: Apply mancozeb or myclobutanil fungicide before bloom. Remove mummified berries and infected leaves.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).treatment: No effective chemical treatment. Remove severely affected vines. Apply wound protectants after pruning.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.treatment: Apply copper-based fungicide or mancozeb. Improve canopy management for air circulation.

Grape___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Continue regular pruning and canopy management.

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).treatment: No cure exists. Control Asian citrus psyllid vector with imidacloprid. Remove and destroy infected trees. Plant disease-free nursery stock.

Peach___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericide. Use resistant varieties. Avoid overhead irrigation.

Peach___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Continue proper pruning and pest management.

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericide with mancozeb. Use disease-free seeds and rotate crops.

Pepper,_bell___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Maintain consistent watering and fertilization.

Potato___Early_blight.treatment: Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb fungicide. Rotate crops and remove plant debris. Ensure proper spacing.

Potato___Late_blight.treatment: Apply metalaxyl or chlorothalonil fungicide immediately. Destroy infected plants. Avoid overhead irrigation.

Potato___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Continue hilling and proper irrigation management.

Raspberry___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Continue regular pruning and trellising.

Soybean___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Maintain proper crop rotation and soil health.

Squash___Powdery_mildew.treatment: Apply potassium bicarbonate or neem oil spray. Ensure good air circulation. Water at the base, not overhead.

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.treatment: Remove infected leaves. Apply copper-based fungicide. Ensure proper plant spacing for air circulation.

Strawberry___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Continue mulching and regular runner management.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericide with mancozeb. Use disease-free transplants. Avoid working with wet plants.

Tomato___Early_blight.treatment: Apply chlorothalonil or copper-based fungicide spray. Mulch around plants. Remove lower infected leaves.

Tomato___Late_blight.treatment: Apply metalaxyl or chlorothalonil fungicide immediately. Remove and destroy infected plants. Avoid overhead watering.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.treatment: Improve greenhouse ventilation. Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb fungicide. Reduce humidity levels.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.treatment: Apply chlorothalonil or copper-based fungicide. Remove infected lower leaves. Mulch to prevent soil splash.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.treatment: Spray with neem oil or insecticidal soap. Increase humidity around plants. Introduce predatory mites as biological control.

Tomato___Target_Spot.treatment: Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb fungicide. Remove infected plant debris. Ensure proper spacing.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.treatment: No chemical cure. Control whitefly vectors with imidacloprid or reflective mulch. Use resistant varieties. Remove infected plants.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.treatment: No chemical cure. Remove infected plants. Disinfect tools with 10% bleach solution. Use resistant varieties and virus-free seeds.

Tomato___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Continue regular staking, pruning, and fertilization.
